sn.s: Compré un ventilador la semana pasada.
sn.e: I bought a fan last week.

sn.s: Pero ya no funciona. Está roto.
sn.e: But it doesn't work anymore. It's broken.

sn.s: Vuelvo a la tienda con el recibo.
sn.e: I go back to the store with the receipt.

sn.s: "Buenas. Compré este ventilador y ya no sirve."
sn.e: "Hello. I bought this fan and it no longer works."
"Do you have the receipt?" the employee asks.

sn.s: "Sí, aquí está. Lo compré hace una semana."
sn.e: "Yes, here it is. I bought it a week ago."

sn.s: "Quiero un cambio o que me devuelvan el dinero."
sn.e: "I want an exchange or my money back."
"Let me check the warranty," she says.

sn.s: Hablo con calma, pero soy firme.
sn.e: I speak calmly, but I'm firm.

sn.s: "Creo que es un reclamo razonable," agrego.
sn.e: "I think it's a reasonable complaint," I add.
"You're right. We'll give you a new one."
"I appreciate it very much," I respond.

sn.s: Reclamar bien no es ser grosero.
Es defender lo que es justo, con respeto.
sn.e: It's standing up for what's fair, with respect.
